name: Kimi CLI
description: Moonshot AI's open-source terminal agent for software-development and terminal tasks; skills and MCP support.

developer.comment: Built by Moonshot AI (the team behind the Kimi models). The repo/product is being rebranded from "Kimi CLI" to "Kimi Code CLI" as of mid-2026; same team and repo (MoonshotAI/kimi-cli). Checked 2026-06-05.

first-release.comment: Repository created 2025-10-15; earliest published release tag (0.33) on 2025-10-21. First public release October 2025.

status.comment: Actively developed (release 1.47.0 by Jun 2026; ~8.9k GitHub stars). Repo is being rebranded to "Kimi Code CLI" but is not discontinued. Checked 2026-06-05.

license.comment: Apache-2.0 (per repo licenseInfo and LICENSE file). The agent tool is open source; the default Kimi models it drives are not.

specialization.comment: Broad terminal coding/dev agent; not positioned for a narrower niche.

interface.comment: Terminal CLI agent with a built-in shell mode (Ctrl-X). Can also run as an ACP server for IDE clients, but it is fundamentally a CLI tool, not an IDE/extension of its own.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal. Via ACP (`kimi acp`) it can be driven from Zed/JetBrains, but it is not itself a host IDE; primary host is the terminal.

open-source.comment: Agent source is Apache-2.0 licensed and publicly developed.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Installed as a standalone CLI (uv tool / pip).

default-model-provider.comment: Default/flagship is Moonshot's own Kimi K2 (Moonshot is not a defined tag option), but the tool also configures OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Vertex, and OpenAI-compatible providers, so provider is effectively multiple.

model-choice.comment: Supports multiple providers (Kimi/Moonshot, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Vertex) selectable via config or --model.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Can authenticate with a Moonshot AI Open Platform API key or other providers' own API keys (OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini).

local-model-support.comment: Supports OpenAI-compatible / custom model endpoints, which covers local servers (e.g. Ollama, vLLM) exposing an OpenAI-compatible API.

multi-file-editing.comment: Reads and edits code across the codebase as part of a task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Can "autonomously plan and adjust actions during execution" — chains edit/run/fix steps toward a goal; supports a --yolo/--afk auto-approve mode for hands-off runs.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands as part of its loop, plus an explicit shell mode (Ctrl-X).

mcp-support.comment: Shipped MCP support; servers managed via `kimi mcp` (add/list/remove/auth/test), HTTP and stdio.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Supports subagents (coder/explore/plan types) launched via an Agent tool for focused subtasks.

codebase-indexing.comment: No documented semantic index/embeddings store; reads files on demand into context rather than building a repo index.

planning-mode.comment: Explicit plan mode (--plan) restricts the agent to read-only tools for analysis before execution.

multimodal-input.comment: ReadMediaFile tool reads image (and video) files when the selected model declares image input capability.

browser-computer-use.comment: Can search and fetch web pages (text), but no documented ability to drive a real browser/GUI to act and verify changes.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Subagents (coder/explore/plan) are launched by the main agent itself as an `Agent` tool call — the model decides mid-task whether to delegate a focused subtask; users cannot invoke subagents directly via a slash command. This is model-driven fan-out, so true per the guideline. subagents-parallel-agents is already true. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Interactive terminal chat with per-action approval (supervised) by default; --yolo/--afk enable autonomous hands-off runs. No tab autocomplete or detached cloud/async execution.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal agent; no as-you-type tab completion.

background-async-execution.comment: Runs in the foreground terminal session; --afk auto-approves prompts but is not a detached/cloud background dispatch where the user closes the session.

persistent-memory.comment: Sessions can be resumed/continued (--session ID / --continue), restoring prior conversation context across runs; AGENTS.md provides persistent project instructions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Provides a checkpoint-rollback mechanism (SendDMail tool described for checkpoint rollback scenarios).

git-integration.comment: No documented dedicated git tooling (staging/diff/commit awareness); git is only reachable by running shell commands, which does not meet the native-git-awareness bar.

pr-creation.comment: No documented native PR creation; could only happen via ad-hoc shell/`gh` invocation, not a built-in feature.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Auto-loads an AGENTS.md project instruction file into the system prompt (the repo itself ships an AGENTS.md).

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP servers, reusable Skills (with a skill-creator), and an Agent SDK / ACP server interface. No dedicated event-hook system documented, but it has clear extension surfaces.

permission-model.comment: Default interactive mode prompts for file edits, shell commands, and network actions; --yolo bypasses approvals and --afk auto-dismisses them, so the posture is configurable.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Open-source CLI that runs entirely on the user's machine; with a local/OpenAI-compatible endpoint it can run without a vendor cloud. No SaaS control plane required for the agent itself.

privacy-mode.comment: No explicit zero-training guarantee documented for the agent/provider. Running against a local or third-party (BYOK) provider avoids Moonshot entirely, but no vendor privacy-mode claim was found; left null. Checked 2026-06-05.

enterprise-controls.comment: No enterprise governance features (SSO/audit/RBAC/VPC) documented for this open-source CLI; could not confirm a 'none' positively from docs, so left null. Checked 2026-06-05.

pricing-model.comment: The CLI tool itself is free and open source. Model usage requires a provider: Moonshot Open Platform API keys are pay-as-you-go (usage-based), or any other provider's key / local model (BYOK). No subscription for the tool. Checked 2026-06-05.

free-tier.comment: The CLI is free; with a free local/OpenAI-compatible model it is usable at no cost. Moonshot's paid API is needed only for the hosted Kimi models.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: No paid plan for the tool; it is free/open-source and can run on BYOK or a local model. Hosted Moonshot model usage is usage-based, not a fixed monthly plan.

maturity.comment: Released Oct 2025, rapidly iterating (1.47.0 by Jun 2026, ~8.9k stars) and mid-rebrand to Kimi Code CLI; usable but still evolving. Checked 2026-06-05.

community-size.comment: ~8.9k GitHub stars as of Jun 2026 — a growing but still single-vendor, relatively new tool compared with Aider/Cline-scale communities.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Supports the broader MCP server ecosystem and shareable Skills (including reuse of .claude/skills directories).

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md as the project instruction file (repo ships its own AGENTS.md).

memory-scopes.comment: Config and skills exist at user level (~/.kimi) and project level (.kimi / AGENTS.md). No enterprise or per-folder scoping documented.

scoped-rules-files.comment: AGENTS.md is an always-applied instruction file; no documented path/glob or file-type conditional rule activation.

auto-memory.comment: Sessions can be resumed and AGENTS.md is a manual instruction file, but no documented mechanism where the agent autonomously writes cross-session memories. Checked 2026-06-05.

skill-support.comment: First-class Skills: a skill is a directory with a SKILL.md; built-in skills (kimi-cli-help, skill-creator) plus user/project skills.

skill-invocation.comment: Users invoke skills via /skill:<name> and /flow:<name>; skill names and descriptions are injected into the system prompt so the model can also select them autonomously.

always-on-surface.comment: On start, Kimi discovers skills and injects their names, paths, and descriptions into the system prompt; full body is pulled in only when invoked (progressive disclosure).

skill-distribution.comment: Skills are directories dropped into user/project skill folders (incl. reuse of .claude/skills); no curated marketplace or package registry documented. Git clone into those folders is possible but not an official distribution channel.

sandboxing-technology.comment: No OS sandbox, container, VM, or filesystem jail documented. The Shell tool executes commands directly via the system shell with the user's full privileges, relying on per-action approval prompts rather than isolation. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Approval is requested per individual action: each shell command execution, each file write/edit, each MCP tool call, and each background-task stop. Reads of non-sensitive files are not gated while writes/commands are (read/write distinction). No command-prefix/path/domain allowlist patterns documented. Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: No declarative allow/deny rule engine documented; the default flow prompts for every gated action, so there is no precedence model. Bypass is only the global --yolo/--afk switch. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: Configuration is limited to mode-level presets (default per-action approval vs --yolo/--yes auto-approve vs --afk). No user-authored declarative allow/deny rules and no programmable permission hooks/policy scripts documented. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: Built-in by default: the ReadFile tool rejects reading sensitive files (.env, SSH private keys, cloud credentials) and the Grep tool always filters them out (even when include_ignored is set), regardless of mode. No user deny rule required. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: No mechanism documented for saving approvals (no 'always allow this command' rule written to durable config); each gated action is approved individually. The only way to stop repeat prompts is the global --yolo/--afk bypass. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: Documented full-autonomy bypasses: --yolo (-y/--yes/--auto-approve) auto-approves all tool calls, and --afk additionally auto-dismisses AskUserQuestion for away-from-keyboard runs. Docs warn all file edits and shell commands then run automatically. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: No outbound-network gating documented. SearchWeb/FetchURL operate without filtering, allowlisting, or proxy controls; since execution is not sandboxed, shell commands also have unrestricted egress. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box (no --yolo/--afk), every shell command, file write/edit, and MCP tool call requires explicit per-action approval. Checked 2026-06-12.
